description_long: This object is a detailed botanical model of a flower mounted on a round black base, with a slender, dark stem supporting the bloom. The flower features several elongated, pale yellowish-brown petals that are slightly curved and arranged in a whorled pattern around the central reproductive structures. The central part of the flower displays prominent, upright, reddish-pink filaments, likely representing stamens, surrounded by bulbous, white to light gray structures that resemble anthers or ovary segments. Some petals and floral parts are partially open, revealing the intricate internal anatomy of the flower. The leaves are lanceolate and attached near the base of the flower, adding to the naturalistic appearance. The overall design suggests a scientific or educational model, possibly for display in a botanical garden or classroom.
description: A realistic flower model with pale, elongated petals, upright reddish filaments, and a dark stem on a round base. The flower's structure is intricate, with visible internal and external floral parts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flower's central filaments, curved petals, and attached leaves, with the base and stem clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated petals and the prominent upright filaments, with more of the flower's internal structure exposed.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the petals and leaves, with the central filaments partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the curvature of the petals and the arrangement of the flower's reproductive structures.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, radiating symmetrically around the central axis of the flower. There is some overlapping and layering, with petals curving outward and partially enclosing the reproductive structures.
